postData.contentHtml:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is slated for a crucial six-day visit to three key Indo-Pacific nations: Indonesia, Australia, and New Zealand, from July 6th to July 11th. This tour underscores India's proactive 'Act East' policy and its commitment to forging stronger alliances in a geopolitically vital region.

The itinerary begins in Indonesia, where discussions are expected to center on deepening bilateral ties, particularly in areas of Indo-Pacific cooperation and defense. This leg of the visit is seen as a continuation of recent high-level engagements, aiming to solidify a strategic partnership with the Southeast Asian giant.

Following Indonesia, Prime Minister Modi will travel to Australia, a crucial Quad partner. While specific details of the engagements are still emerging, the visit is anticipated to further strengthen the comprehensive strategic partnership between India and Australia, covering various sectors including trade, defense, and regional security.

The final leg of the tour will take Prime Minister Modi to New Zealand. This visit marks a significant moment, being the first by an Indian Prime Minister in four decades, highlighting the growing momentum in the New Zealand-India relationship. Discussions are expected to cover trade, investment, maritime security, education, and technology.

Overall, the visit is poised to reinforce India's diplomatic footprint in the Indo-Pacific, emphasizing shared interests in a free, open, and inclusive region. The social sentiment surrounding the trip has been largely positive, reflecting the importance and anticipation of these high-level discussions.
postData.title: India's Diplomatic Outreach: PM Modi Embarks on Key Indo-Pacific Tour
postData.summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to undertake a significant six-day diplomatic tour from July 6-11, visiting Indonesia, Australia, and New Zealand. This strategic outreach aims to bolster bilateral ties, with a particular focus on enhancing Indo-Pacific cooperation and strengthening defense relations in the region.

sources.title: India's Modi to visit Indonesia, Australia and New Zealand next week
PM Modi to visit Indonesia, Australia, New Zealand from July 6-11
PM Modi's Indonesia visit puts Indo-Pacific, defence ties in focus
PM Modi on six-day tour of Indonesia, Australia, New Zealand from July 6
